Expect WR-first at 1.12 (Tyson or Concepcion if he slips), a TE or RB swing at 2.12 (Stowers/Singleton), and an RSP-favored upside flier at 3.12. With only $2.4M of cap space, all three picks likely route to taxi at 50% hits to preserve flexibility for veteran re-signings.

topTargets.name: Malachi Fields
topTargets.reasoning: RSP DoT 87.2 Starter grade with Nico Collins comp; likely on the board at 1.09 after Tate/Lemon/Love come off. Geeks are WR-heavy (9) but their affinity is medium so they'll still take a top-10 RSP board talent over a need pick if BPA is this clear.

topTargets.name: Emmett Johnson
topTargets.reasoning: RSP RB3 (DoT 84.6) addresses the thin RB room (only 4 rostered). Sliding ADP makes him plausible at 1.09 or as a trade-back target; cap-friendly rookie deal fits the $5.9M room.

topTargets.name: KC Concepcion
topTargets.reasoning: Starter grade (DoT 86.3) and ADP 13.57 puts him in range at 1.09. Medium RSP affinity means they trust the board; consensus and RSP align here, lowering risk.

topTargets.name: Demond Claiborne
topTargets.reasoning: RSP DoT 84.5 with Shady McCoy comp — fits as a 2.09 lottery ticket on a thin RB room. ADP slips him to round 2 due to weight concerns, exactly the value Geeks chase with medium RSP lean.

topTargets.name: Eli Stowers
topTargets.reasoning: RSP TE2 (DoT 84.4) with ADP 21.25 lines up as a 3.09 target. Geeks only have 2 TEs and 5 contracts expiring — adding a cheap rookie TE on a multi-year deal hedges roster turnover.

briefs.capPosture: Tight but workable at $5.9M space; can active-roster all three rookies if they stay in expected slots, with one possible taxi stash to preserve flexibility against the 5 expiring contracts.

wildcard.name: Fernando Mendoza
wildcard.reasoning: RSP QB1 (DoT 85.6) at 1.09 would be a reach, but Geeks roster only 2 QBs with contracts churning. Medium RSP affinity could tip them toward Waldman's top overall prospect if WR/RB runs clear the board.

briefs.summary: Gridiron Geeks enter 1.09 with a glaring positional imbalance — 9 WRs but only 4 RBs and 2 TEs — and $5.9M of cap room to absorb rookie deals. Expect them to take the best available RB/TE/WR per their blended RSP+consensus board, with Fields, Johnson, or Concepcion as the realistic 1.09. Rounds 2-3 will target a thunder back (Claiborne) and a developmental TE (Stowers) to stabilize the depth chart against 5 expiring contracts.

topTargets.name: Makai Lemon
topTargets.reasoning: At 1.03 with Tate (RSP #1) and Love (RSP #2) almost certainly off the board, Lemon is the cleanest RSP/consensus overlap available — RSP #3 (DoT 88.8, Starter grade, Amon-Ra St. Brown comp) and ADP 11.18. Medium RSP affinity means I trust the board when RSP and ADP agree, and $10.5M cap space comfortably absorbs a 1.03 contract.

topTargets.name: Malachi Fields
topTargets.reasoning: RSP #4 (DoT 87.2, Nico Collins comp) and a top-10 ADP target. Size/speed boundary WR profile fits a roster already 7-deep at WR but thin on long-term anchors with 6 contracts expiring. Strong fallback if Lemon goes 1.02.

topTargets.name: Jeremiyah Love
topTargets.reasoning: Long shot at 1.03 (ADP 3.59, RSP #2 with All-Pro upside, Kamara comp) but if Dead Cap Walking surprises at 1.02 with a non-RB, Love is the obvious pivot. RB7 on the roster is misleading — most are flex bodies, not anchors, and Love is a true RB1 building block.

topTargets.name: Fernando Mendoza
topTargets.reasoning: RSP QB1 (DoT 85.6) and ADP 11.39 — value at 1.03 if all three top WRs are gone. Six QBs rostered makes this redundant in a vacuum, but with 6 contracts expiring this year, a long-term QB asset slots in cleanly and trade value at QB1 is real if I don't keep him.

topTargets.name: KC Concepcion
topTargets.reasoning: 2.03 target — RSP #5 (DoT 86.3, Starter grade) and ADP 13.57. Best chance of a top-6 RSP WR sliding into round 2 if the room chases consensus names like Tyson and Mendoza. Slot/flanker versatility plus return-game value is exactly the medium-affinity sweet spot.

briefs.capPosture: Comfortable at $34.5M used / $10.5M space — can active-roster all three picks (1.03, 2.03, 3.03) without taxi pressure, though 6 expiring contracts mean I need rookies who can play, not just dev assets.

wildcard.name: Eli Stowers
wildcard.reasoning: 3.03 dart — RSP #11 (DoT 84.4) move TE with QB-convert athletic profile and ADP 21.25 lining up perfectly with the pick slot. TE depth chart is 4 but aging/expiring, and a swing at a developmental TE with Starter-tier upside fits the medium-RSP blend.

briefs.summary: Cowboy Up is picking 1.03 with healthy cap and a roster that needs long-term skill anchors as 6 contracts expire. The plan is to take the best WR available — Lemon if Dead Cap Walking goes RB at 1.02, Fields otherwise — then attack value WRs in round 2 (Concepcion) and a developmental upside swing in round 3. Medium RSP affinity steers me toward names where RSP and ADP agree, avoiding pure consensus reaches like Tyson.

topTargets.name: Fernando Mendoza
topTargets.reasoning: RSP QB1 with DoT 85.6 and ADP 11.39 — sits right at the 1.11 turn. With only 3 QBs rostered and a 1-year contract expiring, Mendoza is the cleanest BPA + need overlap available.

topTargets.name: Emmett Johnson
topTargets.reasoning: RSP RB3 (DoT 84.6) and only 3 RBs on roster. Goes off-board at 1.11 vs ADP but medium RSP affinity gives Waldman's 'underrated talent' tag real weight here.

topTargets.name: Skyler Bell
topTargets.reasoning: RSP WR4 with Ja'Marr Chase high-end comp at DoT 85.9. Doesn't appear on MFL ADP top 30, meaning he'll be available — classic blended-board reach for a Starter-grade WR.

topTargets.name: Jordyn Tyson
topTargets.reasoning: Industry favorite at ADP 12.71 — very likely on the board at 1.11. Adds to an already deep WR room (7) but consensus value is hard to pass on with $5.1M cap and only 1 pick.

topTargets.name: Demond Claiborne
topTargets.reasoning: RSP RB4 (DoT 84.5) with Shady McCoy traits. Falls past 1.11 on most boards but addresses RB-3 depth and fits taxi-eligible pricing if cap forces a low-base contract.

briefs.capPosture: Tight at $39.9M used / $5.1M space — a single round-1 rookie fits on the active roster but a $1M+ base salary likely forces taxi placement to preserve flexibility.

wildcard.name: Eli Stowers
wildcard.reasoning: Already deep at TE (5) so logic says pass, but RSP DoT 84.4 with classic move-TE profile and a former QB pedigree is the kind of value a medium-RSP-affinity GM with one pick might gamble on if the QB/RB tier breaks badly.

briefs.summary: DMOC enters with a single bullet at 1.11 and a cap-tight roster that's WR/TE-heavy and thin at QB and RB. The most likely play is Fernando Mendoza if he slides — addressing the expiring contract and 3-deep QB room — with Emmett Johnson or Demond Claiborne as the RB pivot if a contender jumps Mendoza. Expect taxi placement to manage the $5.1M cap squeeze.

topTargets.name: Malachi Fields
topTargets.reasoning: Most likely best WR on board at 1.06 after Tate/Lemon likely gone (ADP 9.46/11.18). RSP DoT 87.2 Starter grade with Nico Collins comp aligns with both RSP and consensus boards — exactly the type of pick a medium-RSP-affinity GM blends to.

topTargets.name: KC Concepcion
topTargets.reasoning: Likely available at 1.06 if Fields is gone. RSP DoT 86.3 Starter grade and ADP 13.57 give consensus-plus-RSP overlap. Versatile flanker/slot fits a WR room that already has 7 bodies but lacks a clear young alpha.

topTargets.name: Eli Stowers
topTargets.reasoning: Premier 1.17 target — only 2 TEs on the roster makes TE the clearest positional need. RSP DoT 84.4 Rotational Starter on cusp of Starter tier with classic move-TE profile, ADP 21.25 means he'll likely still be there at 1.17.

topTargets.name: Jordyn Tyson
topTargets.reasoning: If he slides to 1.06 (ADP 12.71) the value is too good to pass — boom-bust profile but RSP Rotational Starter at 84.5 DoT. Fits a $2.9M cap squeeze better as a taxi stash than a high-floor active piece.

topTargets.name: Nicholas Singleton
topTargets.reasoning: Round 2 (2.06) target despite having 6 RBs — RSP DoT 84.1 with elite recruiting pedigree, ADP 25.83 means he'll be available. With 4 contracts expiring this year, RB depth turnover is plausible and Singleton offers second/third-round value per RSP.

briefs.capPosture: Tight at $2.9M cap space against $45M cap — 1.06 rookie minimum salary likely forces a taxi designation (50% hit) or shedding an expiring veteran before activation.

wildcard.name: Kenyon Sadiq
wildcard.reasoning: If Stowers is gone by 1.17, Sadiq becomes the TE pivot — RSP DoT 82.3 with Brock Bowers high-end comp and elite Combine numbers (ADP 13.11 suggests he could be a reach at 1.17 but a steal at 2.06 if he falls). Doubles down on the TE-is-thin reality.

briefs.summary: Running down the Dream enters with balanced positional counts but a clear TE deficit (only 2) and a $2.9M cap crunch that forces creative roster construction. Expect a best-WR-available swing at 1.06 (Fields or Concepcion), a deliberate TE grab at 1.17 (Stowers preferred, Sadiq as fallback), and developmental upside picks in rounds 2-3 that can sit on taxi at half-price while expiring contracts roll off.
